trade.summary: Energy dominates the bilateral trade relationship. Italy imports Norwegian natural gas and oil, which became particularly strategic after Russia's invasion of Ukraine prompted Italian diversification. Italy exports machinery, fashion goods, food, and chemicals. Norway's sovereign wealth fund holds significant Italian assets.

diplomatic.summary: Italy and Norway are NATO allies with warm relations. Norway is not an EU member but participates in the EEA. Diplomatic ties are strong and frequent within NATO and other multilateral frameworks. Italy and Norway have occasionally differed on migration policy and fiscal issues but share fundamental Western values.

military.summary: Both are NATO allies. Italy and Norway participate in joint NATO operations and exercises. Both countries have contributed to NATO's eastern flank reinforcement. Italy is an important NATO partner in the Mediterranean; Norway in the Arctic/North Atlantic.

financial.summary: Norway's Government Pension Fund Global holds substantial Italian sovereign bonds and equities — Italy is one of the fund's largest European country exposures. Bilateral investment in energy, shipping, and manufacturing is significant.

energy.summary: Energy is the dominant bilateral economic relationship. Norway is Italy's largest or second-largest gas supplier. After Russia's invasion of Ukraine, Italy rapidly expanded Norwegian gas imports. ENI and Equinor have a deep commercial partnership. Italy also imports Norwegian oil.

scientific.summary: Active scientific cooperation in marine science, Arctic research, and energy technology. Norwegian and Italian research institutions cooperate through NATO Science Programme and bilateral agreements. Joint projects in climate science and renewable energy.
